title: Qualcomm in Talks to Acquire AI Chip Startup Tenstorrent for Up to $10 Billion
excerpt: Qualcomm is reportedly in talks to acquire AI chip design company Tenstorrent for somewhere between $8…
body:
Qualcomm is reportedly in talks to acquire AI chip design company Tenstorrent for somewhere between $8 billion and $10 billion, according to The Information.

Who is Tenstorrent

Founded in 2016, Tenstorrent designs AI accelerator chips built around the open RISC-V instruction set architecture, rather than proprietary designs like Arm or x86. The company is led by Jim Keller, a well-known chip engineer whose past work includes stints at Apple and overseeing Tesla’s efforts to build its own self-driving chip.

Why Qualcomm wants it

Qualcomm has built its business primarily around chips for phones and other end-user devices. A Tenstorrent acquisition would be a clear signal of Qualcomm trying to push further into the data center AI chip market — the upstream side of the AI boom where Nvidia currently dominates — rather than staying focused only on the devices that eventually run AI models.

Why it matters

If completed, this would be one of the largest AI chip acquisitions of the year, and would hand Qualcomm a RISC-V-based accelerator design team with real engineering pedigree just as more chip buyers look for alternatives to Nvidia’s ecosystem. Nothing has been finalized yet, and terms could still change.

Source: Yahoo Finance, citing The Information.
